title: Stisseria conspurcata
intro: Stisseria conspurcata, also known as the 'Milkweed Orchid', is a species of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stisseria conspurcata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stisseria conspurcata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues.
